recipe.title: Jools’s favourite beef stew
recipe.description: Everyone loves a hearty dinner, and this beef stew recipe totally hits the spot. Packed with tender beef, potatoes, loads of veg and fresh herbs, it's rib-stickingly good winter fare.

ingredients.name: a few sprigs of fresh sage
½ a bottle of red wine
a few sprigs of fresh rosemary

steps.descr: Peel and roughly chop the onion, peel and quarter the...
steps.instructions: Peel and roughly chop the onion, peel and quarter the parsnips and peel and halve the carrots. Deseed and roughly dice the squash, and peel and halve the Jerusalem artichokes (if using). Pick the sage leaves.

on_enter.say: Peel and roughly chop the onion, peel and quarter the parsnips and peel and halve the carrots. Deseed and roughly dice the squash, and peel and halve the Jerusalem artichokes (if using). Pick the sage leaves.

steps.descr: Heat a little oil and the butter in a casserole pan on a...
steps.instructions: Heat a little oil and the butter in a casserole pan on a medium heat, add the onion and sage leaves, then fry for 3 to 4 minutes.

on_enter.say: Heat a little oil and the butter in a casserole pan on a medium heat, add the onion and sage leaves, then fry for 3 to 4 minutes.

steps.descr: Meanwhile, toss the meat in a little seasoned flour,...
steps.instructions: Meanwhile, toss the meat in a little seasoned flour, then add it to the pan with all the vegetables, the tomato purée, wine and stock, then gently stir together. Season generously with black pepper and just a little sea salt.

on_enter.say: Meanwhile, toss the meat in a little seasoned flour, then add it to the pan with all the vegetables, the tomato purée, wine and stock, then gently stir together. Season generously with black pepper and just a little sea salt.

steps.descr: Bring to the boil, place a lid on top, then place in the...
steps.instructions: Bring to the boil, place a lid on top, then place in the oven until the meat is tender – sometimes this takes 3 hours, sometimes 4 – it depends on what cut of meat you’re using and how fresh it is. The only way to test is to mash up a piece of meat and if it falls apart easily it’s ready.

on_enter.say: Bring to the boil, place a lid on top, then place in the oven until the meat is tender – sometimes this takes 3 hours, sometimes 4 – it depends on what cut of meat you’re using and how fresh it is. The only way to test is to mash up a piece of meat and if it falls apart easily it’s ready.

steps.descr: Once cooked, turn the oven down to about 110°C/225°F/gas...
steps.instructions: Once cooked, turn the oven down to about 110°C/225°F/gas ¼ and just hold it there until you’re ready to eat.

on_enter.say: Once cooked, turn the oven down to about 110°C/225°F/gas ¼ and just hold it there until you’re ready to eat.

steps.descr: The best way to serve this is by ladling big spoonfuls...
steps.instructions: The best way to serve this is by ladling big spoonfuls into bowls, accompanied by a glass of French red wine and some really fresh, warmed bread.

on_enter.say: The best way to serve this is by ladling big spoonfuls into bowls, accompanied by a glass of French red wine and some really fresh, warmed bread.

steps.descr: Finely grate the lemon zest, pick and finely chop the...
steps.instructions: Finely grate the lemon zest, pick and finely chop the rosemary and peel and finely chop the garlic, then mix together and sprinkle over the stew before serving. Just the smallest amount will make a world of difference – as soon as it hits the hot stew it will release an amazing fragrance.

on_enter.say: Finely grate the lemon zest, pick and finely chop the rosemary and peel and finely chop the garlic, then mix together and sprinkle over the stew before serving. Just the smallest amount will make a world of difference – as soon as it hits the hot stew it will release an amazing fragrance.
